title: Reviving chickpea production in Nepal through integrated crop management, with emphasis on Botrytis gray mold
abstract: This paper describes the current status of chickpea production and assesses the damage caused by the grey mould disease (Botrytis cinerea) in Nepal. It also discusses the effects of different integrated crop management components (host plant resistance, fungicide management, forecasting model-based fungicide application, row spacing variation, and seed treatment with Rhizobium) in the control of Botrytis grey mould and improvement of chickpea production in the country.
